title: Presented at a open papar session presentation at 2023 WCCBT
tags: conference, oral presentation, WCCBT, addiction, internet gaming disorder, alcohol use disorder, fMRI, computational modeling
excerpt:
I gave a oral presentation at 2023 WCCBT, titled “Shared and distinct neurocognitive features of internet gaming disorder and alcohol use disorder” 

By collaborating with psychiatric department in Yonsei university, we analyzed task fMRI data fro...
content:
I gave a oral presentation at 2023 WCCBT, titled “Shared and distinct neurocognitive features of internet gaming disorder and alcohol use disorder” 

By collaborating with psychiatric department in Yonsei university, we analyzed task fMRI data from individuals with internet gaming disorder, alcohol use disorder, and healthy control while they perfomred the two-stage Markov decision task. 


It was a significant milestone for me as it was my first time presenting at an international conference. I found the experience to be highly rewarding, as I had the change to showcase my research, engage in discussions about my other projects, and interact with researchers from different locations! I am excited to share that we are in the final stages of completing our work and preparing it for submission!

title: Qualifying exam for PhD
tags: Qual, graduation, master
excerpt:
I am delighted to share that I have succesfully passed the PhD thesis qualification test.

I conducted an extensive literature search on my research interest, specifically focusing on addiction. I wrote three comprehensive reviews, each spanning o...
content:
I am delighted to share that I have succesfully passed the PhD thesis qualification test.

I conducted an extensive literature search on my research interest, specifically focusing on addiction. I wrote three comprehensive reviews, each spanning over 30 pages, which explored addictino models, naturalstic fMRI paradigm, and voice data in examining addiction.

Engaging in this literature review allowed me to identify gaps within the existing research and develop innovative ideas for future investigations. I thoroughly enjoyed the process of critically analyzing the literature and contributing to the advancement of knowledge in my field. I am excited to continue building upon this foundation and contribute further to the field of addiction research!

content:
I gave a poster presentation at 2022 SfN, titled “Neuroforecasting exceeds self-reports in predicting cosmetic sales: an fMRI study” 

Check out the abstract here! 


This was my first time attending SfN in-person! 
I thoroughly enjoyed sharing my research, engaging in discussions about my other projects (e.g., naturalistc fMRI paradigm, collection voice data in studying psychopathology), interacting with researchers, and gaining insights into their work. It was a truly enriching experience.

This project is based on the ABCD study, and you could see more details in the preprint.

I had such a meaningful time introducing my research, dicussing my other projects, interacting with researchers working in various places and learning their works!

title: Megazone Tech Talk
tags: Megazone, Industry, AI_retreat
excerpt:
After giving a poster presentation at 2021 AI retreat, I was invited to give a talk at Megazone Tech Talk!

I gave a talk on my master theis, “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudinal study...
content:
After giving a poster presentation at 2021 AI retreat, I was invited to give a talk at Megazone Tech Talk!

I gave a talk on my master theis, “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudinal study design and computational modeling” 

It was a great opportunity to introduce my research, and also get to know about Megazone. They are not only providing cloud service. I felt like they are providing AI-based consultation to other industries, such as wine recommendation system, or showing the influence of blankpink on YG entertainment’s building by aggregating all types of SNS around the world. Interesting!!

The conferenece was held in-person, finally! It has been almost 2 years since the last in-person conference. Presenting my research to people in other fields, and talking with people from industries (Naver Clova, Megazone, Kakao etc) were such a great opportunity to expand my field of view :)

I presented my thesis work, “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudinal study design and computational modeling” in 2021 Society for Neuroscience (SfN) Annual Meeting. I orignially planned to attend in-person meeting,  but it has been canceled due to the COVID-19 :( 
However, I enjoyed the discussions with our group members, sharing our researches and motivaion via virtual poster session! :)
If you have registered for 2021 SfN, please checkout the poster session! You could check my posters and also 5 min recordings (Day two, P778 - Modeling and Behavior III #03)

title: Workshop for Motivational Interviewing
tags: motivational interviewing, workshop, clinical psychology, counseling
excerpt:
I took the workshop for Motivational Interviewing by Prof. Sung-Man Shin. 
The lecture addressed ‘motivation’ based on the self-determination theory by Richard Ryan &amp; Edward Deci. 
It was interesting that the theory defines an intrinsic motiva...
content:
I took the workshop for Motivational Interviewing by Prof. Sung-Man Shin. 
The lecture addressed ‘motivation’ based on the self-determination theory by Richard Ryan &amp; Edward Deci. 
It was interesting that the theory defines an intrinsic motivation as a combination of autonomy, belonging, competence, and a sense of goal. 
After learning these theoretical backgrounds, we could practice motivation interviewing in the practice session.
I tried the role of a counselor, and I practiced the OARS techniques (Open questions, Affirming, Reflection, and Summarizing). 
I look forward to applying what I’ve learned in this workshop at the 1:1 counseling session that I’m going to do in the On-campus counseling center :)

tags: Evidence-based treatment, PTSD, workshop, clinical psychology, counseling

The lecture first addressed real examples of PTSD patients, and then discussed how to respond in certain situations...
content:
I took the workshop for Evidence-based trauma treament held by Dr. Hye Sun Joo in Korea Trauma Research &amp; Education Institute. 
The lecture first addressed real examples of PTSD patients, and then discussed how to respond in certain situations with specific exampels. 
Since I’ve never experienced PTSD patients before, this workshop will be helpful for me to work as a counselor at the on-campus counseling center.

From January 04 to January 11, I’ve taken the “Math for Data Science” course of the Data Science Bootcamp held by SNU Graduate School of Data Science.

title: Working at the on-campus counseling center
tags: clinical psychology, counseling, practice consultant, clinical experience
excerpt:
Starting this January, I’m working at the on-campus counseling center at Seoul National University, “Center for Campus Life &amp; Culture”. 
As a graduate student majoring in clinical psychology, this would be a great opportunity to experience rea...
content:
Starting this January, I’m working at the on-campus counseling center at Seoul National University, “Center for Campus Life &amp; Culture”. 
As a graduate student majoring in clinical psychology, this would be a great opportunity to experience real clinical settings, and apply what I learned in lectures so far. 
From January to June, I’ll be doing the intake interview every week :) 
I’m also going conduct some 1:1 counseling sessions! 

As a practice counselor, I hope I could learn a lot and be a helpful counselor to my future clients!

title: Paper review about Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents (Buchel et al., 2017)
tags: presentation, drug use, SUD, addiction, prediction, fMRI, neuroimaging, IMAGEN
excerpt: I gave a presentation on the paper “Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents” by Buchel et al. (2017). I believe predicting future drug use with longitudinal data could...
content:
I gave a presentation on the paper “Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents” by Buchel et al. (2017). I believe predicting future drug use with longitudinal data could help identify the mechanism of substance use/misuses such as risk factors, and further lead to effective early interventions. Since I’m working with other large-sample longitudinal data ( ABCD project ), and looking forward to do such prediction, this paper was a great reference. Also, I hosted a class discussion in the seminar in psychopathology, and we mainly talked about “socioeconomic factors that we should consider when investigating substance use” and “effective intervention approach considering possible side effects”.

Date: 12 October, 2020  
Event: Seminar in Psychopathology 
Location: Seoul, South Korea 
Paper:  Link 
Slides:  Download pdf

title: Paper review about Neural basis of video gaming - a systematic review (Palaus et al., 2017)
tags: presentation, drug use, SUD, addiction, prediction, fMRI, neuroimaging, IMAGEN
excerpt: I gave a presentation on the paper “Neural basis of video gaming: a systematic review” by Palaus et al. (2017). Addiction is an attractive topic, in the respect of irrational decision making. So I decided to present this paper related to the “gami...
content:
I gave a presentation on the paper “Neural basis of video gaming: a systematic review” by Palaus et al. (2017). Addiction is an attractive topic, in the respect of irrational decision making. So I decided to present this paper related to the “gaming disorder”, a topic with an active discussion about whether we should define it as a mental disorder. Together with other two papers discussed in the class, we could think &amp; discuss about the issue of gaming disorder.

title: Presentation on Thomas et al (2020) at Clinical AI journal club
tags: presentation, machine learning, AI, Autism Spectrum Disorder, resting state fMRI, 3D-CNN, ABIDE, journal club
excerpt: I gave a presentation on “Classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder Using the Temporal Statistics of Resting-State Functional MRI Data With 3D Convolutional Neural Networks” by Thomas et al. (2020). The paper tried classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder ...
content:
I gave a presentation on “Classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder Using the Temporal Statistics of Resting-State Functional MRI Data With 3D Convolutional Neural Networks” by Thomas et al. (2020). The paper tried classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) from healthy control using resting-state fMRI, with 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN). Unlike the conventional way of spatial downscaling of fMRI data using brain atlas, the authors tried to reduce the dimensionality of time-domain using multiple summary measures of connectivity! Even though the highest prediction accuracy achieved from multi-measure ensemble approach was not high enough compared to other studies, it was a new perspective of dealing with fMRI data to me!

Date: 22 February, 2021  
Event: CCS lab meeting  
Location: Seoul, South Korea 
Paper:  Link 
Slides:  Download pdf

tags: presentation, decision-making
excerpt:
I gave a talk at “Seminar on Psychology” course in Seoul National University.

The title was “Identifying predictors and mechanisms of maladaptive decision-making”. It was my first time giving a talk introducing my research to undergraduates and o...
content:
I gave a talk at “Seminar on Psychology” course in Seoul National University.

The title was “Identifying predictors and mechanisms of maladaptive decision-making”. It was my first time giving a talk introducing my research to undergraduates and other graduate students. I shared two of my studies: my first-first author paper, Daily caffeinated soda intake as a warning sign indicating later alcohol initiation, and my master thesis, Identifying shaerd and unique mechanisms between food addiction and binge eating based on body image concern.
